title: VinFast, Alcoa, And Regencell Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (March 30-April 2): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: U.S. large-cap stocks posted strong gains during the shortened week of March 30-April 2. VinFast Auto led with a 42.77% jump driven by strong EV momentum and Canadian rebates. Alcoa gained 24.12% on bullish commodities sentiment amid Middle East conflict resolution expectations. Other notable gainers included SBA Communications (23.22%), Equinox Gold (20.98%), and several precious metals miners, with gains ranging from 15-19%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Jumped 42.77% this week driven by strong EV momentum domestically and increased demand for VF 8 in North America boosted by Canadian rebates

title: Why Gold Fields Stock Dropped Today
author: Rich Smith
description: Gold Fields Limited (GFI) stock fell 6.6% as precious metals experienced a sharp selloff. Silver, which hit an all-time high above $80/oz, plummeted to $71.87/oz (-6.9%), while gold dropped 4.4% to $4,352.30. The decline was driven by profit-taking and margin calls from leveraged investors. Despite the pullback, analysts view Gold Fields as attractively valued at 21x trailing earnings with forecasted 50%+ annual earnings growth over five years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock experienced a significant one-day decline (6.6%) due to broader precious metals selloff driven by profit-taking and margin calls. However, the article concludes the stock 'still looks like a buy' based on attractive valuation (21x trailing earnings), strong forecasted earnings growth (50%+ annually), and modest dividend yield (1.3%), suggesting the decline is temporary and not fundamentally driven.

title: Alphabet Crushed Mag 7 Stocks This Year — Only To Be Crushed By These 8 Country ETFs
author: Piero Cingari
description: While Alphabet (Google) led the Magnificent Seven with a 66% year-to-date gain, eight country-focused ETFs delivered even stronger returns, driven by commodity booms in emerging markets and a banking sector revival in Southern Europe. South Korea's iShares MSCI South Korea ETF topped the list with an 87% gain, fueled by AI-driven chipmaker demand, while South Africa, Peru, and Colombia benefited from surging precious metals prices.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 66% year-to-date gain, best performer among Magnificent Seven tech stocks, though outpaced by global country ETFs

title: Montero Secures Option to Acquire Highly Prospective Gold Projects in Chile's Maricunga Belt
author: Globe Newswire
description: Montero Mining and Exploration has secured option agreements to acquire 100% interest in two gold projects (Elvira and Potrero) in Chile's Maricunga Belt, with staged payments totaling US$7 million per project over six years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as an existing mine operator in the Maricunga Belt region
Mentioned as an existing mine operator in the Maricunga Belt region

title: Why Gold Stocks Soared Today
author: Billy Duberstein
description: Gold prices have surged 30.7% this year as geopolitical tensions between the U.S., China, and Russia have fueled a rush to the 'safe haven' precious metal. Gold mining stocks have outperformed, with Iamgold, Gold Fields, and Fortuna Mining rallying significantly on Monday.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions that Iamgold Corp's stock rallied 8.9% on Monday, indicating a positive sentiment due to the surge in gold prices.
The article states that Gold Fields Ltd's stock rose 9.5% on Monday, suggesting a positive sentiment as the company benefits from the increase in gold prices.
The article reports that Fortuna Mining Corp.'s stock jumped 12.1% on Monday, reflecting a positive sentiment due to the strong performance of gold mining stocks.
